zh.lifeSummary: 當Judith Elizabetha Wylley於大約1535年出生在英國素福爾可維斯塔爾時，她的父親Thomas Wale16歲，母親Jane Cooper15歲。她於1565年與Thomas Artys在英國素福爾可維斯塔爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1592年在英國享年58歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可萊塔姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Judith Elizabetha Wylley于大约1535年出生在英国素福爾可維斯塔爾时，她的父亲Thomas Wale16岁，母亲Jane Cooper15岁。她于1565年与Thomas Artys在英国素福爾可維斯塔爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1592年在英国享年58岁，埋葬在英国素福爾可萊塔姆。

nameMeanings.name: Wylley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Staffordshire, Cheshire, and Lancashire): variant of   Whaley  .

variants.name: Whaley
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from Whalley (Lancashire) or Whaley in Bolsover (Derbyshire). The Lancashire placename may derive from Old English hwæl ‘hill’ + lēah ‘wood, woodland clearing’. The Derbyshire placename may derive from Old English wall ‘wall’, walh ‘Welshman, foreigner’ (genitive plural wala), or wælla ‘well, spring, stream’ + lēah.

nameMeanings.name: Judith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, meaning ‘Jewess’ or ‘woman from Judea’, borne by a Jewish heroine whose story is recorded in the Book of Judith in the Apocrypha. Judith is portrayed as a beautiful widow who delivers her people from the invading Assyrians by gaining the confidence of their commander, Holofernes, and cutting off his head while he is asleep; without their commander, the Assyrians are duly routed. This has been a perennially popular Jewish name. In the English-speaking world it was taken up in the 16th century, having been in occasional use among Gentiles before this: for example, it was borne by a niece of William the Conqueror. It enjoyed great popularity between the 1940s and the 1960s. Today's notable bearers include the American novelist  Judith Krantz  (  b. 1928 ) and the Scottish composer  Judith Weir  (  b. 1954 ).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabetha
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Judith Elizabetha Wylley was born about 1535, in Westhall, Suffolk, England, her father, Thomas Wale, was 16 and her mother, Jane Cooper, was 15. She married Thomas Artys in 1565, in Westhall, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. She died in 1592, in England, at the age of 58, and was buried in Wrentham, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Judith Elizabetha Wylley est née environ 1535, à Westhall, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Wale, avait 16 ans et sa mère, Jane Cooper, avait 15 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Artys en 1565, à Westhall, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée en 1592, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterrée à Wrentham, Suffolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Judith Elizabetha Wylley um 1535 in Westhall, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Wale 16 und ihre Mutter Jane Cooper 15. Sie hat 1565 in Westhall, Suffolk, England Thomas Artys geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb 1592 im Alter von 58 Jahren in England und wurde in Wrentham, Suffolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Judith Elizabetha Wylley è nata il circa 1535 nel Westhall, Suffolk, England, sua padre, Thomas Wale, aveva 16 anni e sua madre, Jane Cooper, aveva 15 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Artys nel 1565, nel Westhall, Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 5 figlie. Morì nel 1592, nel England, all'età di 58 anni, e fu sepolta a Wrentham, Suffolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Judith Elizabetha Wylleyが約1535年にイギリススフォルクウエスサルで生まれた時，父Thomas Waleは16歳，母Jane Cooperは15歳であった。1565年にイギリススフォルクウエスサルでThomas Artysと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1592年にイギリスで58歳で死去し，イギリススフォルクウレンサムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Judith Elizabetha Wylley(이)가 대략 1535년 영국 수폴크 웨스솔에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Wale(은)는 16세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jane Cooper(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 1565년 영국 수폴크 웨스솔에서 Thomas Artys(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1592년 영국에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 렌섬에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Judith Elizabetha Wylley nasceu aproximadamente 1535, em Westhall, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Wale, tinha 16 anos e sua mãe, Jane Cooper, tinha 15 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Artys em 1565, em Westhall, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1592, em Inglaterra, com 58 anos, e foi sepultada em Wrentham, Suffolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Judith Elizabetha Wylley родилась около 1535 г. в местности вестолл, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Wale было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Jane Cooper, 15. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Artys в 1565 г. в местности вестолл, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла в 1592 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 58 лет и была похоронена в местности рентам, суффолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Judith Elizabetha Wylley nació aproximadamente 1535, en Westhall, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Wale, tenía 16 años y su mamá, Jane Cooper, tenía 15 años. Se casó con Thomas Artys en 1565, en Westhall, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió en 1592, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 58 años, y fue sepultada en Wrentham, Suffolk, Inglaterra.
